authorization.scope: Upload only the two public scheduler probes to the authorized Education page.

captureBoundary.loadMethod: Official local-cart input, onchange handler, FileReader, and codo data path
captureBoundary.startMethod: Click the official visible start surface before selecting the cart
captureBoundary.runMethod: Enqueue the four run-command key bytes through the official codo key buffer after the cart load completed
captureBoundary.stdoutMethod: Bind Module.print before the official runtime asset initializes and retain only p8probe events
captureBoundary.repeatability: scheduler_30 was repeated in a fresh page and produced the same first four update/draw pairs byte-for-byte
captureBoundary.mutationExclusion: No cart bytes, runtime logic, clock values, or emitted events were injected or rewritten

defectAndFix.officiallyExposedDefect: The implementation clock started at the first executed update, while the captured first Education 0.2.7 callbacks were consistently one thirtieth of a second later than that update-count-only origin.
defectAndFix.sharedRootCause: Logical update count was incorrectly used as both gameplay callback count and the complete cartridge clock origin.
defectAndFix.sharedFix: The core now keeps the captured startup clock origin separate from logical update count. The Wasm/native host applies the two-tick origin only after _init has been invoked, so the existing synchronous _init clock remains unchanged while the first 30 Hz and 60 Hz callbacks report the captured values.

qualified: first six host ticks of 30 Hz update/draw ordering
first six host ticks of 60 Hz update/draw ordering
time() progression over the captured boundaries
notQualified: time() values observed inside _init or during a suspended _init
historical or licensed desktop runtime versions
